pattern: Union-Find where each number is unioned with all its prime factors, grouping numbers that share any common factor into connected components.
hints: Two numbers belong to the same component if they share any prime factor; use Union-Find to merge them transitively.
For each number, factorize it and union the number with each of its prime factors (using a shifted index space to separate primes from numbers).
After all unions, count how many numbers map to each root; the largest count is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: all pairwise coprime, each its own component
two coprime groups of equal size
large prime numbers that are all coprime
number 1 is coprime with everything so stays isolated
power of same prime chains all together

stress.description: random unique positive integers up to 100000
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(20000, max(1, size))
    vals = rng.sample(range(1, 100001), n)
    return [vals]
